overview: Logic for joining external event data against r/ForbiddenBromance subreddit archive

priority_windows.description: Time windows most likely to show event-discourse correlation

windows.rationale: Ceasefire signing triggers normalization discourse, peace-branding, immediate violations trigger blame debates
Pager attacks, Nasrallah assassination, ground invasion - maximum subreddit activity spike documented
Senior Hezbollah commander killed, major retaliation - documented activity spike
War begins, Hezbollah opens front - sets tone for entire discourse period
Direct normalization talks, Beirut strike - documented activity spike, normalization discourse peak
Beirut strikes resume, ceasefire effectively ends - documented recent spike
Palestinian refugee camp strike, 11 children killed - test for Palestine-topic suppression

tier_1_triggers.expected_response: High activity, celebration in anti-Hezbollah circles, debate over proportionality
Intense activity, historical 2006 comparisons, sectarian narratives
Immediate blame-framing debates, violation tracking, state failure narratives

tier_2_triggers.expected_response: Moderate activity, depends on casualties and location
Variable, targeted killings trigger more discussion than overflights

tier_3_triggers.expected_response: Low activity unless part of larger pattern
Low activity unless framed as systematic destruction

expected_subreddit_patterns: Blame on Hezbollah for dragging Lebanon into war
Blame entire Shia community for Hezbollah
Calls for Christian/Sunni alliance against Shia
Positive references to Lebanese Forces, Kataeb, Phalange
Support for direct talks with Israel
Absence of discussion when Palestinian casualties occur
Rapid topic changes away from Palestine
Framing Palestine as separate from Lebanon
Calls for stronger state against Hezbollah

obscure_name_logic.description: How to detect suspicious mention of obscure names/places

detection_rules.logic: If subreddit post/comment mentions obscure village (WATCH_V_*) within 24 hours of event, flag as HIGH SUSPICION
detection_rules.rationale: Obscure villages rarely make English/international news - immediate mention suggests local Lebanese monitoring or inside information

detection_rules.rule_name: Multiple obscure names in single post
detection_rules.logic: If post/comment mentions 3+ obscure watchlist items, flag as HIGH SUSPICION
detection_rules.rationale: Detailed geographic/personal knowledge unusual for casual observer

detection_rules.logic: If obscure name spelled with correct transliteration matching NNA/Arabic sources, flag as MEDIUM SUSPICION
detection_rules.rationale: Suggests direct Arabic source access rather than English media

detection_rules.logic: If mentions specific Hezbollah units (e.g., Nasr Unit) or command structure, flag as MEDIUM SUSPICION
detection_rules.rationale: Organizational detail suggests military interest or inside knowledge

detection_rules.logic: If celebrates killing of obscure Hezbollah member (WATCH_P_* with low public profile), flag as HIGH SUSPICION
detection_rules.rationale: Celebrating obscure figure suggests tracking Hezbollah personnel systematically

local_vs_global_source_weighting_logic.description: How to assess whether subreddit discussion matches source accessibility

rules.scenario: Event only reported in NNA (Arabic) or local Lebanese outlets
rules.expected_subreddit_behavior: Minimal discussion unless users actively monitor Lebanese sources
rules.suspicion_trigger: Immediate detailed discussion suggests organized monitoring

rules.scenario: Event reported in English wire services (AP, Reuters, Al Jazeera English)
rules.suspicion_trigger: None, unless details only in Arabic sources appear

rules.scenario: Event reported only in Arabic regional outlets (Al Mayadeen, Al Jadeed)
rules.suspicion_trigger: Any discussion suggests Arabic source monitoring or coordination
Specific framing that matches Israeli government/IDF messaging

temporal_joins.method: Match event_date to subreddit post/comment date (exact day)
Match event_date ± 1 day to subreddit activity
temporal_joins.use_case: Catch delayed reactions or time zone differences
temporal_joins.sql_example: WHERE post_date BETWEEN event_date - INTERVAL 1 DAY AND event_date + INTERVAL 1 DAY

temporal_joins.method: Match event_date ± 3 days to subreddit activity
temporal_joins.use_case: Catch discussion waves and follow-up threads
temporal_joins.sql_example: WHERE post_date BETWEEN event_date - INTERVAL 3 DAY AND event_date + INTERVAL 3 DAY

temporal_joins.method: Group events and posts by YYYY-MM
temporal_joins.use_case: Correlate monthly event density with monthly activity spikes

entity_joins.method: Match people_named field to subreddit post/comment text (fuzzy match)
entity_joins.use_case: Detect mentions of specific people from events
entity_joins.implementation_notes: Use LIKE or full-text search; account for spelling variations

entity_joins.use_case: Detect obscure village mentions (CRITICAL for suspicion detection)
Detect discussion of Hezbollah units, IRGC, LAF, etc.

semantic_joins.method: Match incident_type to inferred subreddit post topic
semantic_joins.use_case: Correlate event types (assassinations, airstrikes) with discussion patterns
semantic_joins.implementation_notes: Requires topic modeling or keyword extraction on subreddit text

semantic_joins.method: Match topic_labels to subreddit discourse categories
semantic_joins.use_case: Test whether anti-Hezbollah events trigger anti-Hezbollah posts
semantic_joins.implementation_notes: Apply topic classifier to subreddit content, join on labels

special_markers.method: Flag events on symbolic dates (e.g., Nasrallah death anniversary)
special_markers.use_case: Detect whether subreddit activity spikes on symbolic dates
special_markers.implementation_notes: Create calendar of key dates, join on date

special_markers.use_case: Test whether normalization events trigger peace-branding discourse
special_markers.implementation_notes: Filter where incident_type = 'diplomacy' or topic_labels contains 'normalization'

data_quality_checks: Verify date_precision matches actual date format
cross_reference_checks: All watchlist event_ids exist in event datasets
All watchlist items have valid source_urls
Watchlist village names match event location fields
Watchlist person names match event people_named fields

analysis_priorities: PRIORITY 1: Obscure village mentions on subreddit within 24-48 hours of NNA-only reporting
PRIORITY 2: Celebration of Hezbollah member deaths where victim is obscure (not widely covered)
PRIORITY 3: Correlation between violation patterns and anti-Hezbollah discourse spikes
PRIORITY 4: Palestine-topic suppression pattern: absence of discussion when Palestinian casualties occur
PRIORITY 5: Normalization discourse alignment with diplomatic events
PRIORITY 6: Sectarian framing correlation with Christian militia historical events
PRIORITY 7: Timing analysis: immediate vs delayed reactions (coordination indicators)
PRIORITY 8: Source language patterns: Are users citing English sources or showing knowledge only available in Arabic?
PRIORITY 9: Identity consistency: Do users claiming Lebanese identity show knowledge patterns inconsistent with claimed location?
PRIORITY 10: Hostility spikes: Do certain event types trigger disproportionate hostility or celebrate violence?
